rulings.Case Title: IDA CHRISTINA FUA CRUZ VS. JOEL ENRIQUE ANDINO SANCHEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT TAXI EQUIPMENT LEASING LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The statute of limitations does not bar the third cause of action because it relates back to the original complaint. The relation back doctrine applies where (1) the new cause of action is based on the "same general set of facts" as the original; (2) seeks recovery against the same defendants for the "same injuries" and (3) refers to the "same incident" - i.e., the "same accident" caused by the "same offending instrumentality." (Barrington v. A.H. Robins Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 146, 150; Weil & Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) 6:735.) Here, the facts in the original complaint and the First Amended Complaint are identical, both complaints seek recovery against the same defendants for the same injuries, and both complaints refer to the same accident caused by the same offending instrumentality. Further, "in applying the relation-back analysis, courts should consider the strong policy in this state that cases should be decided on their merits." (Pointe San Diego Residential Community, L.P. v. Procopio, Cory, Hargreaves & Savitch, LLP (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 265, 277.) The Court did not consider the Declaration of Mark D. Rosenberg in ruling on the demurrer. A demurrer can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or matters judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: IDA CHRISTINA FUA CRUZ VS. JOEL ENRIQUE ANDINO SANCHEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT TAXI EQUIPMENT LEASING LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. In its notice of motion, Defendant first seeks to strike the language in paragraph 29 of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") as follows: "Defendants Taxi Equipment Leasing¿recklessly and negligently owned, operated, and/or controlled the Nissan Taxi Cab driven by Sanchez." However, paragraph 29 of the FAC states "Plaintiff incorporates herein by reference, as though set forth in full, Paragraphs 1 through 28 of this Complaint." Therefore, the motion as to this language is denied as improperly noticed. As to the alter ego allegations, the motion is denied. "California courts have a policy of great liberality in allowing amendments at any stage of the proceeding." (Douglas v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 155, 158.) It serves judicial economy to allow the amendment to stand, rather than require Plaintiff to file a motion for leave to amend, which would likely be granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARK BIRKHEAD VS. OLIVER PARKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Order;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF MARK BIRKHEAD'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Discovery Order. Denied. Plaintiff did not file or serve opposition to Defendants' Motion to Compel Further Response to Defendants' First Request for Production of Documents that was heard on April 3, 2014. Plaintiff received the tentative ruling prior to the hearing and affirmatively communicated to both the Court and opposing counsel that he would not appear or contest the tentative ruling. "The tentative ruling will become the ruling of the court if the court has not directed oral argument by its tentative ruling and notice of intent to appear has not been given." (CRC Rule 3.1308(a)(1).) Further, Plaintiff did not appear at the hearing and is deemed to have stipulated to the attorney serving as a temporary judge. (CRC 2.816(d)(1).) Accordingly, Plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing to obtain relief pursuant to CCP § 473 and any relief pursuant to CCP § 1008 has long expired. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DGP ASSOCIATES LP VS. AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Special Interrogatories, Set Three; Motion For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DGP ASSOCIATES LP Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Special Interrogatories, Set Three; Motion For Award Of Monetary Sanctions (Part 1 of 2 tentative ruling) Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS SET FORTH BELOW: 1 DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 68, 69 & 70. DEFENDANT REPRESENTS THAT IT HAS PRODUCED ALL NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS REFLECTING COMMUNICATION BETWEEN IT AND ANY REINSURERS. IT WOULD IMPOSE AN UNREASONABLE BURDEN ON DEFENDANT TO HAVE TO "IDENTIFY" EACH DOCUMENT SPECIFICALLY DESCRIBED IN THE INTERROGATORY WHEN SUCH IDENTIFICATION CAN BE DONE BY PLAINTIFF FROM THE FACE OF THE DOCUMENT OR FROM THE CONTENTS OF THE PRIVILEGE LOG PROVIDED BY DEFENDANT. 2 DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 71, 72 & 73, THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THE INFORMATION SOUGHT IS RELEVANT TO ANY ISSUE IN THE LITIGATION NOR IS LIKELY TO LEAD TO DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. 3 DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 74. DEFENDANT HAS ASSERTED THAT ALL COMMUNICATION BETWEEN IT AND COUNSEL ARE PRIVILEGED COMMUNICATIONS AND ARE REFERENCED IN THE PRIVILEGE LOG PROVIDED. 4 GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 75. DEFENDANT WILL FILE A RESPONSE WITHIN 15 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 5 DENIED AS TO PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ("RFP") NO. 17 EXCEPT THAT DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS REFLECTING INSURERS REQUESTS FOR INFORMATION THAT IT HAS WITHHELD ON THE BASIS THAT THEY ARE NOT REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. IF A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE IS ASSERTED DEFENDANT WILL IDENTIFY WHERE ON THE PRIVILEGE LOG SUCH DOCUMENTS ARE INCLUDED OR IF NOT ON THE PRIVILEGE LOG THEY WILL BE IDENTIFIED WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALLOW A CHALLENGE TO SUCH DESIGNATION. 6 DENIED AS TO RFP NOS 18, 19, 20, 21 & 22 FOR THE REASONS SET FORTH IN PARAGRAPHS 1, 2 & 3 ABOVE. 7 DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE WITHIN 15 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER COPIES OF ALL ATTACHMENTS REFERENCED IN IT COMMUNICATIONS TO ITS REINSURERS. IF SUCH DOCUMENTS HAVE PREVIOUSLY BEEN PRODUCED, DEFENDANT WILL IDENTITY SUCH PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED DOCUMENTS BY BATES NUMBER.(end of part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling)=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GRETCHEN LEMAISTRE VS. CHRONICLE BOOKS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Further Answrs To Interrogatories And Compelling Response To Request For Production Of Documents And Compelling Response To Request For Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF GRETCHEN LEMAISTRE's Motion For Order Compelling Further Answrs To Interrogatories And Compelling Response To Request For Production Of Documents And Compelling Response To Request For Admissions And For Sanctions (part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF GRETCHEN LEMAISTRIE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANDT IS ORDERED TO SERVE PLAINTIFF FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION SAVE AS TO ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE, TO ALL DISCOVERY REQUESTS TO WHICH IT OBJECTED BASED ON THE GROUND THAT THE REQUEST EXCEEDED THE LIMITATIONS SET FORTH IN CIVL CODE SECTION 94(a). DEFENDANT UNILATERALLY CHOSE TO TREAT THE ACTION AS A MATTER OF LIMITED JURISDICTION EVEN THOUGH THE PLEADINGS ON THEIR FACE INDICATED THAT IT WAS NO SO LIMITED. IF DEFENDANT DESIRED TO HAVE THE MATTER SUBJECT TO THE LIMITATIONS ON DISCOVERY APPLICABLE TO A COURT OF LIMITED JURISDICTION IT COULD HAVE TAKEN STEPS TO HAVE THAT DETERMINATION TESTED. TO THE EXTENT THAT RESPONSES WERE GIVEN TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 1,2, 3, 4, 5 & 6 DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO ADVISE PLAINTIFF IF IT IS WITHOLDING ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ON THE BASIS OF ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE AND TO PROVIDE A PRIVILEGE LOG SUFFICIENT TO ENABLE PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THAT DESIGNATION. AS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 9 - 17 DEFENDANT WILL SERVE ON PLAINTIFF FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES AFTER IT HAS RESPONSED TO PLAIINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES TO WHICH THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DIRECTED. WITH RESPECT TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, DEFENDANT WILL SERVE PLAINTIFF WITH RESPONSES WHICH CONFIRM TO THE REQUIREMENTS OF CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SE.2033.220- EITHER ADMIT, DENY OR UNABLE TO ADMIT OR DENY AFTER REASONABLE INQUIRY ALL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES WILL BE SERVED ON PLAINTIFF WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000 ARE ASSSESSED AGAINST DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL, PAYABLE WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR AGREEMENT AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON JUNE 26, 2014.(end of part 1 of 2)=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LITHIUM TECHNOLOGIES, INC VS. LEAH GROSKY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT LEAH GROSKY'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings. Granted, in part, and denied, in part. For Section (a), the arbitration costs of 20% allocated to Defendant as the employee is substantively unconscionable. (Ontiveros v. DHL Exp. (USA), Inc. (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 494, 510.) The arbitration costs shall be borne on Plaintiff employer. For Section (b), the arbitration length is neither procedurally nor substantively unconscionable. For Section (c), the provision is procedurally unconscionable. (Trivedi v. Curexo Technology Corp. (2010) 189 Cal.App.4th 387, 397.) However, Plaintiff may seek provisional relief pursuant to CCP § 1281.8. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GANADERIA BRASIL INDUSTRIA E COMERCIO DE ACESSORIO VS. LEVI STRAUSS & CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS LEVI STRAUSS & CO. and MARYANN NICHOLS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is overruled as to Defendant Nichols. Individual employees are immune from liability for corporate wrongs only if they do not authorize, direct, or in some meaningful sense actively participate in the wrongful conduct. (Asahi Kasei Pharma Corporation v. Actelion Ltd. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 945, 966.) The Demurrer is overruled as to causes of action one, five, and six. Defendants admit that they are not a parties to the contract at issue and do not assert themselves as agents of any contracting party, and they are therefore "strangers" to the contract. (Id. at 963-64.) The Demurrer as to the second, third, and fourth causes of action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff to properly allege that Defendant is either a party to the contract or is liable under the alter ego doctrine. Finally, the Demurrer to the eighth cause of action is overruled as a Complaint may state a cause of action for injunctive relief. In light of the ruling on the Demurrer, the Court need not rule on the petition to compel arbitration and stay the other causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GANADERIA BRASIL INDUSTRIA E COMERCIO DE ACESSORIO VS. LEVI STRAUSS & CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Protective Order Requiring Pltfs To Return Privileged Documents And Prohibiting Futher Use Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS LEVI STRAUSS & CO. and MARYANN NICHOLS' Motion For A Protective Order Requiring Plaintiffs To Return Privileged Documents And Prohibiting Further Use Thereof. Defendant's motion for a protective order, immediate return of all attorney-client/attorney-attorney work-product privileged documents identified in its privilege log, and prohibition of use of the privileged documents, is granted. The communications in question are unquestionably covered by the attorney client/attorney work product privileges. (Evid. Code § 954.) Defendant did not waive said privileges based on the communications with plaintiff's co-counsels during the period December 24, 2013 through February 7, 2014. (Evid. Code § 912.) It has long been the rule the "gotcha" theory of waiver as the equivalent of actual waiver is not a voluntary release of privileged documents. (See State Compensation Ins. Fund vs. WPS, Inc., (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 644.) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE KIHAGI VS. KELLY KIMBALL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kelly Kimball'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT KELLY KIMBALL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Plaintiff failed to comply with the notice provisions outlined in Civil Code section 1946.1(h). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
